question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted StreamFileSignature as the first element of the collection with an artificially low getTotalStake() value so that stakeRequiredForConsensus is computed from a manipulated baseline, causing a minority-stake hash group to falsely reach consensus and accept a tampered Hashgraph history file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where all entries have getStatus() != VERIFIED so that signatureHashMap remains empty, then exploit the BigDecimal.ZERO.equals(consensusRatio) bypass path (line 54) to make validate() return successfully without any consensus check, allowing a tampered stream file to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause an integer overflow in the stake accumulation variable (stake += signature.getNode().getStake()) by supplying signatures whose individual getStake() values sum beyond Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the computed stake to wrap to a large negative number that satisfies canReachConsensus() and falsely validates a tampered history file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with a null fileHash so that getFileHashAsHex() returns null, causing all null-hash signatures to be grouped under the null key in signatureHashMap, and if enough such signatures exist they collectively satisfy canReachConsensus(), allowing a tampered file with no valid hash to be accepted as consensus-reached?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a signatures collection where the first signature's node returns getTotalStake() == 0, triggering a SignatureVerificationException with message 'Invalid total staking weight', and use this as a denial-of-service vector to permanently block ingestion of legitimate Hashgraph history files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that totalStake is read exclusively from the first signature's node (signature.getNode().getTotalStake()) and never cross-validated against other nodes, by ordering the collection so a node with an inflated totalStake appears first, thereby raising stakeRequiredForConsensus so high that legitimate consensus is never reached, causing a denial-of-service on Hashgraph history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that totalStake is read exclusively from the first signature's node and never cross-validated, by ordering the collection so a node with a deflated totalStake appears first, thereby lowering stakeRequiredForConsensus so that a minority-stake attacker-controlled hash group satisfies canReachConsensus() and a tampered Hashgraph history file is accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple VERIFIED signatures all sharing the same getFileHashAsHex() value (attacker-controlled hash) but with individually small getStake() values that collectively exceed stakeRequiredForConsensus, causing the attacker's chosen file hash to reach CONSENSUS_REACHED status and a tampered stream file to be ingested as legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HashMultimap grouping by getFileHashAsHex() to split legitimate node signatures across multiple distinct hash keys (by injecting signatures with slightly different fileHash bytes), diluting each group's stake below stakeRequiredForConsensus, so that no legitimate hash group reaches consensus and the importer is forced to skip or reject valid Hashgraph history files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user pre-set a StreamFileSignature's status to SignatureStatus.VERIFIED before passing it to validate(), bypassing the NodeSignatureVerifier.verifySignature() cryptographic check entirely, so that an unsigned or forged signature is treated as verified and contributes to consensus for a tampered Hashgraph history file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() uses BigDecimal.ZERO.equals(consensusRatio) (object equality) rather than compareTo(BigDecimal.ZERO) == 0, by supplying a consensusRatio of new BigDecimal('0.00') (scale 2) which is numerically zero but not .equals(BigDecimal.ZERO), causing the bypass condition to fail and the validator to proceed with a near-zero threshold that accepts a tampered file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: canReachConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a negative getStake() value for a node (e.g., via a crafted ConsensusNode implementation), causing BigDecimal.valueOf(stake).compareTo(stakeRequiredForConsensus) to return a negative result that still satisfies the >= 0 check if stakeRequiredForConsensus is also negative, leading to false consensus on a tampered Hashgraph history file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where all nodes have getStake() == 0 and getTotalStake() == 0 (triggering the zero-stake fallback path in ConsensusNodeWrapper), causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to throw SignatureVerificationException with 'Invalid total staking weight', and use this as a repeatable denial-of-service to block all Hashgraph history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a single VERIFIED signature whose node has getStake() equal to getTotalStake() (i.e., one node holds 100% of stake), causing canReachConsensus() to return true for a single-node collection, so that a tampered Hashgraph history file is accepted based on a single unverified node's signature?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that consensusCount is incremented by validatedSignatures.size() (signature count) rather than by stake weight, so that a group with many low-stake signatures passes the consensusCount > 0 check even if the actual stake-based canReachConsensus() check was borderline, leading to acceptance of a tampered Hashgraph history file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where the first signature has a null getNode() reference, causing a NullPointerException when accessing signature.getNode().getTotalStake() on line 45, and use this as a denial-of-service to crash the importer's Hashgraph history ingestion pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where a non-first signature has a null getNode() reference, causing a NullPointerException during the stake accumulation loop (stake += signature.getNode().getStake()), crashing the validation and potentially leaving the importer in an inconsistent state regarding Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where different nodes report inconsistent getTotalStake() values, and since validate() only reads totalStake from the first node, exploit this inconsistency to manipulate the stakeRequiredForConsensus threshold and cause a tampered Hashgraph history file to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where all signatures have status DOWNLOADED (not VERIFIED), causing signatureHashMap to remain empty, and then if consensusRatio is BigDecimal.ZERO the bypass at line 54 is NOT triggered (because signatureHashMap.isEmpty() is true), resulting in a SignatureVerificationException — but can this be chained to force the importer to skip a legitimate Hashgraph history file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a totalStake value of Long.MAX_VALUE, causing BigDecimal.valueOf(totalStake).multiply(consensusRatio) to produce a result that overflows or loses precision when converted back, resulting in an incorrect stakeRequiredForConsensus threshold that allows a minority-stake group to falsely satisfy canReachConsensus() for a tampered Hashgraph history file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection that is a mutable list and concurrently modify it during the iteration in validate(), causing a ConcurrentModificationException or race condition that skips signature verification and allows a tampered Hashgraph history file to pass consensus validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() mutates the status of StreamFileSignature objects in-place (s.setStatus(CONSENSUS_REACHED)) to cause a race condition where a signature's status is read as CONSENSUS_REACHED by the downstream Downloader.verifySignatures() before the full consensus check completes, leading to acceptance of a tampered Hashgraph history file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where two different hash groups each have exactly stakeRequiredForConsensus stake, causing both groups to reach CONSENSUS_REACHED status, and then exploit the fact that Downloader.verifySignatures() iterates over all CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures to force ingestion of the attacker's chosen tampered Hashgraph history file over the legitimate one?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a signatures collection where the same node appears multiple times (duplicate ConsensusNode entries), causing its stake to be counted multiple times in the stake accumulation loop, inflating the computed stake beyond the actual network stake and falsely satisfying canReachConsensus() for a tampered Hashgraph history file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileSignature with getFileHashAsHex() returning an empty string '', causing all such signatures to be grouped under the empty-string key in signatureHashMap, and if enough such signatures exist they collectively satisfy canReachConsensus(), allowing a tampered file with an empty hash to be accepted as consensus-reached?
